SLB to share requirements on what they are looking for' per Next_Step), and Veronica requested a demo of the supplier-facing UI for tail-spend autonomous negotiation (Next_Step commitment). However, NO quantified business case has been captured: no cost reduction target, no cycle-time savings in days, no headcount freed, no error-rate improvement, and no ROI projection tied to the $1 billion spend volume mentioned. Without a dollar-anchored metric, the CFO (Stephane Biguet, Executive Sponsor) has no financial justification to approve the $250k investment, and Zycus risks losing to a competitor who builds the business case first. The April urgency ('POC slash pilot pretty fast… within weeks') signals high pain, but the subsequent silence and lack of quantified value suggest either buyer de-prioritization or that another vendor is further ahead in demonstrating ROI.

competition.narrative: SLB is actively evaluating multiple vendors in parallel and has stated that Zycus is late to the process. The Competitors__c field lists 'SAP Ariba;Others,' and the buyer explicitly stated on 16 April (per prior sweep risk record) 'that project, it's already moving very fast, and we're in talks with suppliers already. You guys are coming a little late in the picture,' which confirms that at least one other autonomous sourcing vendor (likely a specialized AI/ML procurement vendor or an Ariba-native extension) is further ahead in technical validation or POC. SAP Ariba is listed as the primary competitor, and given SLB's Ariba/S4HANA environment, a competitor with native Ariba integration or an Ariba Guided Buying extension for autonomous sourcing would have a significant technical advantage and lower implementation risk. The buyer requested POC 'within weeks' in April, but Zycus has not delivered a demo or moved to POC 10+ weeks later, which means competitors have had 10+ weeks to advance their technical validation, build internal advocacy, and lock in the pilot. The fact that the buyer has gone quiet ('no update - have gone quiet' 16 June) after expressing urgency in April strongly suggests that another vendor is now in POC or preferred-vendor status, and Zycus is being kept warm as a backup. The wedge to win: Zycus must demonstrate superior autonomous negotiation intelligence (Merlin/ANA agentic AI capability that goes beyond rule-based automation) and prove seamless Ariba/S4 integration in a live demo or POC within the next 7–14 days, AND secure a champion who can advocate for Zycus's differentiation internally. If Zycus does not re-engage and move to POC immediately, the competitor will lock in and Zycus will be out.

identify_pain.narrative: SLB's operational pain centers on sourcing velocity and tail-spend automation: the need to eliminate manual RFQ cycles for repetitive low-value price-list purchases, and to trigger autonomous renegotiation when contracts expire without waiting for a new purchase requisition. Julian Howley (Process Improvement Leader, Global Procurement) articulated the autonomous sourcing workflow and use case on the 22 April call ('good call with Julian bikash and devika. SLB to share requirements on what they are looking for' per Next_Step), and Veronica Gouvea (Supply Chain Performance Manager, the Evaluator role) requested a demo of the supplier-facing UI specifically for tail-spend autonomous negotiation scenarios, which confirms that tail-spend process inefficiency is a live pain point she owns. The prior sweep risk record states that SLB is 'in talks with suppliers already' and that the autonomous sourcing project 'is already moving very fast' (16 April buyer quote), which signals high urgency and active pain driving the initiative. However, the pain has NOT been quantified: no cost impact, no cycle-time baseline (e.g. 'manual RFQs take 14 days, we want 2'), no headcount burden stated, and no error rate or compliance risk articulated. The fact that the buyer went silent after April urgency ('no update - have gone quiet' 16 June) suggests either that the pain has been addressed by another vendor or internal workaround, or that the pain owner (Veronica, Julian) lacks the executive sponsorship to move the initiative forward and is waiting for leadership buy-in.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics, legal review process, procurement channel, InfoSec requirements, or SI/partner involvement have been discussed or documented in any call, Next_Step note, task, or Salesforce field. There is no evidence of MSA/SOW scoping, no mention of legal or security review gates, no InfoSec questionnaire or compliance requirements captured, and no indication of whether SLB procurement flows through a centralized sourcing team, a procurement portal, or requires supplier onboarding/registration. The Products field lists 'iContract;iSource;Merlin;Agentic AI;ANA,' which suggests a multi-module SaaS deal, but no implementation partner, SI involvement, or services scope has been mentioned. The prior sweep risk record identifies Ariba/S4HANA integration as a must-have technical gate, which implies ERP and procurement-system integration will be part of implementation, but the actual implementation mechanics (deployment timeline, IT/architecture review, data-migration scope, training, go-live plan) have not been validated. The next move is to map the paper process explicitly in the re-engagement conversation: ask who owns contracting (procurement, legal, IT), what the approval chain looks like (single-signature vs multi-level), whether InfoSec/compliance review is required, and what the implementation and onboarding timeline would be post-signature.

decision_criteria.narrative: SLB's evaluation criteria for the autonomous sourcing solution have been partially articulated through the 22 April call with Julian, Bikash, and Devika ('good call with Julian bikash and devika. SLB to share requirements on what they are looking for' per Next_Step), where SLB committed to share a written requirements document but has not yet delivered it. From prior sweep records and Next_Step commitments, the confirmed technical criteria include: (1) seamless integration with SAP Ariba to pull PRs and trigger workflows on contract expiration (not just PR creation), (2) seamless integration with S/4HANA ERP to push awards as outline agreements, (3) ability to ingest SLB's homegrown regression-model target pricing as an external data source to guide autonomous negotiations, (4) autonomous negotiation capability for tail-spend price-list scenarios with a supplier-facing UI, and (5) speed to POC/pilot ('we would like to get in a POC slash pilot pretty fast… within weeks' per prior sweep). Veronica requested a prerecorded demo showing the supplier-facing UI for tail-spend autonomous negotiation, which signals that user experience and supplier adoption are implicit criteria. However, the FULL decision criteria (weighting, must-haves vs nice-to-haves, evaluation scorecard, other vendors' feature comparison) have not been documented or validated in any call or field, and the buyer's written requirements document remains undelivered 10+ weeks after commitment. Without the complete criteria and without a delivered demo or POC, Zycus cannot confirm fit or differentiate against competitors who may already be further ahead in technical validation.

The buyer requested a demo of the supplier-facing UI for ANA's autonomous negotiation scenario, which confirms interest in AI/ML-driven automation beyond rule-based workflows. However, the AI story is under-positioned because the demo has not been delivered and no POC has been run to validate the AI capability. Tier: AI Hungry (articulated need for agentic AI autonomous sourcing), but the deal is at risk because AI differentiation has not been demonstrated and competitors may be further ahead in AI validation.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal cooling after strong April engagement; buyer has gone quiet for 20 days despite three undelivered Zycus commitments and urgent POC timeline stated in April. Re-engagement required within 7 days to prevent competitor lock-in.

flags: Time-to-action is the single highest risk: Julian stated 16 April 'we would like to get in a POC slash pilot pretty fast… within weeks' and 'you guys are coming a little late in the picture', and it has now been 10+ weeks since that call with no demo delivered and no POC scheduled. Other vendors are further ahead ('we're in talks with suppliers already'), and if Zycus does not re-engage and move to POC within the next 7–14 days, another vendor will lock in the pilot and Zycus will be out. The activation: schedule the follow-up demo THIS WEEK (by 3 July), deliver the written use-case recap and POC plan by end of next week (by 5 July), and propose a 2–3 week POC timeline to replicate SLB's autonomous sourcing workflow in the Zycus environment, proving Zycus can execute faster than the competition.

Economic Buyer and project-lead gap: the deal is being driven by evaluators (Veronica, Julian) and influencers, but the real sponsors (project leads Juliana and Natalia, named by Julian as 'driving' the initiative) and the Economic Buyer (Stephane Biguet CFO listed as Executive Sponsor, or another procurement exec with budget authority) have not been engaged or introduced. Without exec-level sponsorship and access to the true decision-makers, Zycus is at risk of losing on technical evaluation alone while a competitor with stronger internal advocacy wins the commercial and strategic conversation. The next move: ask Julian (or re-engage Veronica) to introduce Zycus to Juliana, Natalia, and the budget decision-maker, and anchor the value conversation at that level with a quantified ROI business case (sourcing velocity ROI, tail-spend automation savings, headcount efficiency) that justifies the $250k investment and positions Zycus as the strategic partner, not just a feature vendor.

Competitive differentiation lever: Zycus's agentic platform configurability (user-configurable workflows and guardrails, no hard-coding, ability to ingest external data like SLB's regression model and adapt to their conditional escalation logic) is the wedge vs. rigid/templated competitors — Bikash demonstrated this live on 16 April and Julian responded 'it looks good'. But this differentiator has not been activated into a POC that PROVES Zycus can replicate SLB's exact workflow (contract-expiration trigger → regression-model target pricing → autonomous RFP/negotiation → second-round bid with rationale → SME escalation → S/4 outline-agreement award push) faster and more accurately than the competition. The activation: in the follow-up demo, walk through a pre-built simulation of SLB's use case in the Zycus environment (using sample contract-expiration data, mock regression-model target prices, and the S/4 integration architecture), show the user-configurable workflow builder and guardrail settings that map to SLB's requirements, and propose a 2-week POC where Zycus ingests SLB's real regression-model output and runs a live autonomous sourcing event end-to-end — make it tangible, make it fast, and make it SLB-specific so the buyer can see Zycus is the only vendor who truly 'gets' their use case and can deliver it at speed.

Undelivered commitments (fatal): Three Zycus-side deliverables from 20 March call (prerecorded ANA demo for Veronica, ballpark SaaS cost for $1B spend, product-team validation on expiration-trigger workflow) remain undelivered 14+ weeks later. Follow-up demo and use-case recap requested 22 April undelivered 10+ weeks later. Buyer has gone quiet ('no update - have gone quiet', 16 June). These missed commitments have destroyed trust and momentum.

No Economic Buyer engagement (fatal at this stage): CFO Stephane Biguet (Executive Sponsor) has not been engaged on any call or task in 102-day history. Project leads Juliana and Natalia (mentioned 16 April as 'driving' the initiative) have not been introduced. All engagement is with evaluators (Veronica, Julian) who lack budget authority. Next_Step 1 April: 'not moving forwrd to share pricing as we need more buy in from SLB side'. Without EB access, this deal cannot progress to commercials or close.

Competitive timing risk (imminent): SLB stated 16 April 'that project, it's already moving very fast, and we're in talks with suppliers already. You guys are coming a little late in the picture' and 'we would like to get in a POC slash pilot pretty fast… within weeks'. 10+ weeks later, no demo or POC delivered, and buyer has gone quiet. Other vendors (SAP Ariba and unnamed competitors) are further ahead in technical validation. If Zycus does not re-engage and move to POC within 7-14 days, another vendor will lock the pilot and Zycus will be out.

No quantified ROI or business case: Operational pain is clear (tail-spend sourcing bottleneck), but no dollar-anchored business case (cost per event, cycle-time reduction, headcount freed, savings target) has been built to justify $250k investment at CFO/exec level. Without a quantified ROI story, the Economic Buyer has no basis to approve budget.

Weak champion (developing, now at risk): Julian Howley is the most engaged contact but has not introduced Zycus to the EB, project leads, or Decision Makers, has not driven urgency against competitors, and has gone quiet in the last 6+ weeks. Veronica Gouvea is similarly engaged but junior (Evaluator role, no budget authority). Without a senior champion (Director or VP-level procurement leader with exec access and political capital), this deal will not progress past evaluation.

Single-threaded: deal is driven by evaluators (Julian Howley, Veronica Gouvea) with no Economic Buyer (Stephane Biguet CFO) or Decision Maker (Laurent Geranton, Michael Scott, Nelma Tavares) engagement in any call or task. No multi-threading at exec level.

If Zycus does not re-engage and move to POC within 7-14 days, another vendor will lock the pilot and Zycus will be out.

No quantified ROI or business case: Operational pain is clear (tail-spend sourcing bottleneck), but no dollar-anchored business case (cost per event, cycle-time reduction, headcount freed, savings target) has been built to justify $250k investment at CFO/exec level. Without a quantified ROI story, the Economic Buyer has no basis to approve budget.

Weak champion (developing, now at risk): Julian Howley is the most engaged contact but has not introduced Zycus to the EB, project leads, or Decision Makers, has not driven urgency against competitors, and has gone quiet in the last 6+ weeks. Veronica Gouvea is similarly engaged but junior (Evaluator role, no budget authority). Without a senior champion (Director or VP-level procurement leader with exec access and political capital), this deal will not progress past evaluation.

Time-to-action is the single highest risk: Julian stated 16 April 'we would like to get in a POC slash pilot pretty fast… within weeks' and 'you guys are coming a little late in the picture', and it has now been 10+ weeks since that call with no demo delivered and no POC scheduled. Other vendors are further ahead ('we're in talks with suppliers already'), and if Zycus does not re-engage and move to POC within the next 7–14 days, another vendor will lock in the pilot and Zycus will be out. The activation: schedule the follow-up demo THIS WEEK (by 3 July), deliver the written use-case recap and POC plan by end of next week (by 5 July), and propose a 2–3 week POC timeline to replicate SLB's autonomous sourcing workflow in the Zycus environment, proving Zycus can execute faster than the competition.

Economic Buyer and project-lead gap: the deal is being driven by evaluators (Veronica, Julian) and influencers, but the real sponsors (project leads Juliana and Natalia, named by Julian as 'driving' the initiative) and the Economic Buyer (Stephane Biguet CFO listed as Executive Sponsor, or another procurement exec with budget authority) have not been engaged or introduced. Without exec-level sponsorship and access to the true decision-makers, Zycus is at risk of losing on technical evaluation alone while a competitor with stronger internal advocacy wins the commercial and strategic conversation. The next move: ask Julian (or re-engage Veronica) to introduce Zycus to Juliana, Natalia, and the budget decision-maker, and anchor the value conversation at that level with a quantified ROI business case (sourcing velocity ROI, tail-spend automation savings, headcount efficiency) that justifies the $250k investment and positions Zycus as the strategic partner, not just a feature vendor.

Competitive differentiation lever: Zycus's agentic platform configurability (user-configurable workflows and guardrails, no hard-coding, ability to ingest external data like SLB's regression model and adapt to their conditional escalation logic) is the wedge vs. rigid/templated competitors — Bikash demonstrated this live on 16 April and Julian responded 'it looks good'. But this differentiator has not been activated into a POC that PROVES Zycus can replicate SLB's exact workflow (contract-expiration trigger → regression-model target pricing → autonomous RFP/negotiation → second-round bid with rationale → SME escalation → S/4 outline-agreement award push) faster and more accurately than the competition. The activation: in the follow-up demo, walk through a pre-built simulation of SLB's use case in the Zycus environment (using sample contract-expiration data, mock regression-model target prices, and the S/4 integration architecture), show the user-configurable workflow builder and guardrail settings that map to SLB's requirements, and propose a 2-week POC where Zycus ingests SLB's real regression-model output and runs a live autonomous sourcing event end-to-end — make it tangible, make it fast, and make it SLB-specific so the buyer can see Zycus is the only vendor who truly 'gets' their use case and can deliver it at speed.

The activation: in the follow-up demo, walk through a pre-built simulation of SLB's use case in the Zycus environment (using sample contract-expiration data, mock regression-model target prices, and the S/4 integration architecture), show the user-configurable workflow builder and guardrail settings that map to SLB's requirements, and propose a 2-week POC where Zycus ingests SLB's real regression-model output and runs a live autonomous sourcing event end-to-end — make it tangible, make it fast, and make it SLB-specific so the buyer can see Zycus is the only vendor who truly 'gets' their use case and can deliver it at speed.
